wasteTaxonomy.wastedGBPNote: £67bn cost growth from £33bn original estimate to £100bn latest (covers both scope-cut Phase 2 and overrun on Phase 1). March 2026 parliamentary report: £43.6bn spent to Feb 2026.
£4bn cost overrun (£14.8bn → £18.8bn), opened 3.5 years late in May 2022. NAO cited governance failure pre-2018 reset.
Cancelled 2017, nothing built. £53.5m public money spent (£43.4m public + £9.4m Treasury underwrite). NAO cited procurement failures.
£30bn+ cost growth (£18bn → £48bn+) with 6-year delay. EDF impairment charges recorded. CfD strike price will burden consumers for 35 years.
NHS National Programme for IT dismantled 2011. Estimated £9.8bn sunk into the failed programme. PAC called it 'one of the worst and most expensive contracting fiascos' in public-sector history.
£13.6bn cost growth from original £2.2bn IT estimate; £34m IT written off in 2013 reset. NAO: 'good news story turned into a serious mess'.
All-lane-running smart motorway rollout cancelled April 2023 for safety reasons after fatalities. £62m spent on 14 schemes that will not proceed. PAC and Transport Committee both critical.
Cancelled July 2024 after repeated legal and environmental challenges. £179m development spend.
E-Borders terminated 2010, successor DABS descoped. Home Office paid Raytheon £150m arbitration settlement 2015. £830m total sunk with minimal delivery.
Cancelled 2010 under SDSR; all 9 airframes scrapped. £3.6bn sunk. NAO 'a catalogue of failure'.

Horizon wrongful-prosecution scandal: £1bn+ redress (HSS, GLO, OHCS, HCRS schemes) + £400m+ inquiry legal costs + historical IT spend with Fujitsu. Post Office Horizon IT Inquiry ongoing; total public cost estimated £2.4bn+ as of FY2024/25.
Cancelled March 2021 after 6 months; £314m spent, only 47k of the planned 600k vouchers redeemed. PAC 'rushed implementation'.
£52bn+ cost growth from original UK estimate, 5-year capability delay, reduced initial buy commitment. NAO/PAC both cited sustainment-cost overruns.
£175m sunk 2011–2022; retired April 2023. Never reached 25m-user target. Replaced by GOV.UK One Login.
£14.4bn cost growth vs original UK estimate; extensive export-support-cost overruns.
Nominal lifetime cost ballooned from ~£45bn to ~£136bn. NAO/PAC repeatedly critical of contracting failures and safety-regulator fines (2023: £332k fine for data-security breach).

wasteTaxonomy.projectName: Connecting Europe Express / Channel Tunnel Upgrades
A428 Black Cat to Caxton Gibbet

wasteTaxonomy.wastedGBPNote: Cancelled March 2021 under Integrated Review. £642m spent with no operational upgrade delivered. NAO: 'culture of optimism bias'.
Retired March 2025 having entered service 8 years late, never reached full operational capability; 5 crashes. £1.35bn spent for marginal capability.
Welsh Government cancelled June 2019 after public inquiry on environmental grounds. £130m spent on development.
£34bn cost growth from original £1bn estimate as scope and timeline extended out to 2050s.
Hitachi suspended 2019 after £2bn invested by developer; UK government unable to agree financing. Site subsequently acquired by Great British Nuclear at 2024 for £160m.
Toshiba/NuGen cancelled 2018; £450m write-off. Sellafield-adjacent site no new nuclear capacity delivered.
£15bn cost growth vs original £22bn budget; PAC 'muddled, overstated, eye-wateringly expensive'. Consultants and contact-tracers on day rates up to £6,600.
£12bn+ cost growth; £8.7bn of PPE written off as unusable or expired; VIP lane referrals under criminal investigation; Good Law Project and NAO both critical. Covid-19 Inquiry Module 5 ongoing.
Barge lease terminated January 2025 with no cost savings vs hotels demonstrated. Legionella discovery and a death on board. NAO critical.
Site works abandoned 2024; no asylum seekers ever housed. Heritage damage and WLDC judicial-review costs.
£251.7m of overpayments owed by ~144k carers after DWP system failed to flag earnings-threshold breaches. Select Committee inquiry ongoing.
Cancelled March 2023 by DfT; £25m development spend sunk.

Northern legs cancelled October 2023 having spent £2.6bn on land acquisition, design and enabling works. NAO and PAC critical of decision-making process and poor value for money on sunk land holdings.
Scheme cancelled July 2024 having removed zero asylum seekers. ~£290m paid directly to Rwanda + £400m+ in UK operational and legal costs. NAO total estimate £700m.

wasteTaxonomy.projectName: COVID Bounce Back Loan Scheme Fraud
